name: Florida v. Jardines
summary: Use of drug-sniffing dog on homeowner's porch to investigate contents of home is search within meaning of Fourth Amendment; curtilage of home receives Fourth Amendment protection.
holding: Law enforcement use of trained police dog to explore curtilage of home to investigate contents constitutes search; government trespass on constitutionally protected area to gather information is Fourth Amendment search.
relevance_to_audit: Establishes strong protection for home curtilage; relevant to analyzing government inspections, physical intrusions, and surveillance at private residences or business premises.
